unit.note: server vitest 178 files / 2085 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local; +2 new CI-gated guard files: deploy-detach.test.ts = caller half, deploy-swap-contract.test.ts = swapper half, 39 tests). client vitest 206 files / 2016 passed (untouched). scripts node --test: 68 passed (deploy-swap.test.mjs new; start-server-production{,.sh}.test.mjs re-pointed at the new contract per the Test-Update-Klausel). tsc --noEmit clean in both workspaces; oxlint clean on the new files.

integration.note: Purpose-built pty harness (throwaway, documented in the iterate spec): the REAL deploy script runs inside a REAL @lydell/node-pty shell hosted by the process that owns the port - the exact topology of the Command Center embedded terminal. Run twice: against the pre-fix script from git HEAD and against the fixed one. This is the only construction that can exercise the bug; a unit test cannot model a ConPTY teardown.

e2e.note: The end-to-end verification for this change IS the pty deploy harness above (not a Playwright spec - the change has no web surface). RED: pre-fix script => caller killed by the ConPTY cascade, NO server on :3947, no status file = the 2026-07-14 outage reproduced exactly. GREEN: fixed script => caller killed just the same, server back (pid 17664), HTTP 200 on /api/diagnostics, deploy-status.json ok:true readiness:listener. Re-run after every rework round, including the full post-review rebuild.

smoke.note: The fixed script was also run directly (no pty, isolated PORT=3947 + temp HOME): build -> hand-off -> swapper -> server up; the caller reported OK from the swapper status file. Confirms the surviving-caller path, which the pty run cannot show (there the caller is dead by design).

surface_verification.justification: Ops tooling only: the diff touches scripts/ (two deploy shell scripts + two new Node helpers) and two server vitest guard files. No route, store mutation, WS handler, message contract, or any code consumed by the UI changed - there is no web surface to drive, so the Backend-affects-Frontend rule does not apply. It was instead verified end-to-end where the bug actually lives: the real deploy script executed inside a real node-pty shell hosted by the process owning the port (the embedded-terminal topology), once against the pre-fix script from git HEAD (caller killed, NO server, no status file - the outage reproduced) and once against the fixed script (caller killed just the same, server back, HTTP 200, deploy-status.json ok:true). Playwright cannot model a ConPTY teardown; this harness is the only proof that exists.

test_completeness.enumeration_basis: The 6 acceptance criteria in .shipwright/planning/iterate/2026-07-14-deploy-self-kill.md, plus every behavior the diff introduces (port contract, kill scope, kill verification, readiness, status file, heal placement, detach mechanics, log degradation), plus every defect the two review rounds surfaced (external LLM review + internal code review) - each became its own row = 19 behaviors.

behaviors.behavior: Caller performs no kill; delegates to the swapper (.ps1 + .sh)
Hand-off happens only AFTER the build (failed build => running server untouched)
EVERY npm install/build step carries an ABORT guard (order alone is not enough)
behaviors.evidence: deploy-detach.test.ts (added after code review: the order-only test stayed green with every guard deleted)

behaviors.behavior: Hand-off is detached (Start-Process / nohup|setsid, stdin from /dev/null)
Hand-off survives a repo path containing spaces
behaviors.evidence: measured: unquoted + spaced path => node 'Cannot find module'; quoted => correct argv

behaviors.behavior: The .ps1 parses under Windows PowerShell 5.1 (the shell the embedded terminal spawns)
behaviors.evidence: 5.1 parser run + ASCII-only-code-lines test in deploy-detach.test.ts

behaviors.behavior: Swapper kills ONE PID, never the tree (/T would kill the swapper itself)
Swapper does NOT start when the port never frees (old server beats no server)
Readiness = OUR child owns the port (a surviving old listener is not success)
Readiness degrades, never fails, where listeners are unobservable (no lsof)
Swapper starts the new server detached (it outlives the swapper)
behaviors.evidence: deploy-swap-contract.test.ts + pty harness: the server outlived the swapper exit

behaviors.behavior: Post-restart claude.json heal runs in the swapper, AFTER readiness (clean window)
A verdict is written in EVERY path, including an unexpected throw
An unopenable log never blocks the server start (EBUSY => degrade)
behaviors.evidence: contract test + reproduced: server started, log skipped with a warning

behaviors.behavior: Caller verdict comes from a FRESH deploy-status.json, never a bare listener
behaviors.evidence: deploy-detach.test.ts (a listener check must not set the success flag)

behaviors.behavior: PORT contract identical in caller(s) + swapper (1-5 digits, > 0, else 3847)
behaviors.evidence: scripts/deploy-swap.test.mjs (units + structural parity on both callers)

behaviors.behavior: Kill scope is the Hono port only - never the Vite port
AC1: the server comes back although the caller is killed mid-deploy
behaviors.evidence: pty harness RED/GREEN: pre-fix => caller killed + NO server; fixed => caller killed + server up (HTTP 200, deploy-status.json ok:true)

behaviors.behavior: The .sh twin executes on macOS (setsid absent => nohup branch)
behaviors.evidence: No macOS host available here. Mitigated: all logic lives in the shared cross-platform Node swapper (unit-tested); the .sh delta is the hand-off line - structurally pinned, bash -n syntax-checked, and the nohup branch is exactly the one macOS takes.
